content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : File                                    Date  : April 11, 1977


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Ken Finney

Subject: Telephone conversation with Norbert Dall, lobbyist for the
         Sierra Club.

        Norbert Dall, a lobbyist for the Sierra Club, called today
and inquired about the legality of engaging in the follow-
ing transaction. A friend of his who now is the executive
director of a state agency is willing to sell Mr. Dall a
typewriter for $75. The state agency is not one which
Mr. Dall attempts to influence and, hence, he has not regis-
tered with respect to that agency. In addition, he does not
anticipate that he will attempt to influence the agency in
the future.

        I informed Mr. Dall that under these circumstances there
is no prohibition against purchasing the typewriter from
the agency official. In addition, I stated that he would
not incur a reporting obligation. This advice was based on
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18600 which states that duties or
prohibitions imposed on lobbyists apply only to those agency
officials of agencies which the lobbyist is registered to
lobby.

KF:jo

sections.question: A friend of his who now is the executive director of a state agency is willing to sell Mr. Dall a typewriter for $75. The state agency is not one which Mr. Dall attempts to influence and, hence, he has not registered with respect to that agency. In addition, he does not anticipate that he will attempt to influence the agency in the future.
sections.conclusion: I informed Mr. Dall that under these circumstances there is no prohibition against purchasing the typewriter from the agency official. In addition, I stated that he would not incur a reporting obligation. This advice was based on 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18600 which states that duties or prohibitions imposed on lobbyists apply only to those agency officials of agencies which the lobbyist is registered to lobby.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a registered lobbyist may purchase a typewriter for $75 from an executive director of a state agency that the lobbyist is not registered to lobby and does not intend to lobby in the future.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: There is no prohibition against the lobbyist purchasing the typewriter and no reporting obligation is incurred because the relevant agency is not one the lobbyist is registered to lobby, and lobbyist duties and prohibitions only apply to agencies where the lobbyist is registered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an internal FPPC memorandum documenting telephone advice rather than a formal written response letter. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers present, but content clearly reflects Q&A structure. Question and conclusion sections extracted from narrative text. Document dated April 11, 1977 with ID 77ADV-77-445.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A friend of his who now is the executive director of a state agency is willing to sell Mr. Dall a typewriter for $75. The state agency is not one which Mr. Dall attempts to influence and, hence, he has not registered with respect to that agency. In addition, he does not anticipate that he will attempt to influence the agency in the future.

CONCLUSION: I informed Mr. Dall that under these circumstances there is no prohibition against purchasing the typewriter from the agency official. In addition, I stated that he would not incur a reporting obligation. This advice was based on 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18600 which states that duties or prohibitions imposed on lobbyists apply only to those agency officials of agencies which the lobbyist is registered to lobby.

Norbert Dall, a lobbyist for the Sierra Club, called today and inquired about the legality of engaging in the follow- ing transaction. A friend of his who now is the executive director of a state agency is willing to sell Mr. Dall a typewriter for $75. The state agency is not one which Mr. Dall attempts to influence and, hence, he has not regis- tered with respect to that agency. embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum documenting a telephone consultation with a Sierra Club lobbyist regarding the legality of purchasing a typewriter from a state agency official. The FPPC advised that no prohibition applies and no reporting obligation is required under Cal. Adm. Code Section 18600 since the lobbyist is not registered to lobby that particular agency.
